title: Chunk curriculum into smaller pieces/chapters with a dependency tree & table of contents in a separate md file so we can parse it quicker
description:
The induction_curriculum.md file is currently 3897 lines and difficult to parse/navigate. Refactor to improve organization:

OBJECTIVES:
- Split curriculum into modular chapter files (one file per lesson or per phase)
- Create master table of contents with dependency tree
- Improve parsability and navigation
- Maintain all existing content

PROPOSED STRUCTURE:
- curriculum/README.md (TOC + dependency visualization)
- curriculum/phase1/ (lessons 01-10)
- curriculum/phase2/ (lessons 11-18)
- curriculum/phase3/ (lessons 19-26)
- curriculum/phase4/ (lessons 27-32)
- curriculum/phase5/ (lessons 33-38)
- curriculum/phase6/ (lessons 39-44)

BENEFITS:
- Faster file parsing (smaller files)
- Better git diffs (changes isolated to specific lessons)
- Easier navigation with TOC
- Clear dependency visualization
- Modular updates without conflicts

REQUIREMENTS:
- Preserve all 68 photo placeholders
- Maintain cross-references between lessons
- Generate dependency graph (e.g., Lesson 28 depends on Lesson 27)
- Include metadata (duration, prerequisites, tools needed)
- Keep original file as reference until migration complete

title: Level 6: Full System Integration & Validation
description: Complete end-to-end system integration connecting all subsystems. Verify full control loop operation, safety interlocks, thermal management, EMI filtering, and system-level performance. Validate against design specifications (1500-2000W, temperature accuracy, ZVS operation, protection response times).

title: Verify complete power delivery path: AC mains to pan heating
description: Simulate full power path integration: AC input → rectifier → DC bus → half-bridge → resonant tank → pan load. Verify end-to-end power conversion efficiency, harmonic distortion (THD), power factor, inrush current, and transient response to load changes. Measure losses in each stage and identify optimization opportunities.

title: Verify complete control loop: sensing to actuation
description: Integrate complete control loop: temperature/current sensing → ESP32-S3 ADC → PID controller → PWM generation → gate drivers → power stage. Verify closed-loop stability, step response, disturbance rejection, sensor-to-actuator latency (<10ms target), and temperature control accuracy (±0.5°C). Reference Lessons 27-31.

title: Verify safety interlock system and fault response
description: Integrate all safety features: overcurrent detection (hardware + software), overtemperature shutdown, UVLO, overvoltage protection, pan detection, watchdog timer. Verify fault detection latency, protection threshold accuracy, safe-state fallback, and fault recovery procedures. Reference Lessons 22, 27, 30.

title: Verify EMI/EMC performance and filtering effectiveness
description: Simulate EMI filter design (common-mode, differential-mode) at AC input. Verify conducted emissions compliance (EN 55014-1), switching noise suppression, ground loop prevention, and shield effectiveness. Measure noise coupling to sensing circuits and verify filtering adequacy. Predict radiated emissions hotspots.

title: Verify system-level thermal management under worst-case conditions
description: Simulate complete thermal system: IGBT losses, LMR51430 thermal, capacitor heating, enclosure temperature rise, fan control. Verify thermal runaway prevention, derating curves, continuous operation at rated power (2kW), and thermal shutdown thresholds. Test worst-case: 85°C ambient, no pan (no-load), maximum power.

title: Verify system meets all design specifications and performance targets
description: Final validation against design requirements: 1500-2000W power range, temperature accuracy ±0.5°C, ZVS operation >90% duty range, efficiency >85%, startup time <5s, temperature settling time <60s, fault response <100ms, isolation >2.5kV, EN 55014 compliance. Generate comprehensive test report documenting compliance.

title: Simulate induction coil thermal losses
description: Simulate coil I²R losses including AC resistance effects at 38kHz. Estimate heat generated in Litz wire at 2kW output. Determine if dedicated coil cooling is needed or if shared airflow with IGBT heatsink is sufficient.

title: Define thermal management BOM and layout
description: Select heatsink, fan, thermal interface materials. Define airflow path in enclosure. Specify NTC thermistor placement for thermal protection. Document in thermal design guide.

title: Generate verification reports for safety simulations
description:
Several safety-critical simulations run successfully but lack formal verification reports:
- sim_17_ocp_protection.cir (OCP verified: 33ns response)
- sim_18_ovp_protection.cir (OVP resistive divider)
- sim_19_thermal_shutdown.cir (NTC thermal protection)
- sim_20_interlock_integration.cir (fault OR gate + latch)

The circuits work correctly but need documentation for traceability.

title: Create KiCad schematic for Temper induction cooker
description:
Set up hierarchical KiCad project with all subsystems:

**Hierarchical Sheets:**
1. Power Input (AC rectification, voltage doubler)
2. Half-Bridge (IGBTs, gate driver, bootstrap)
3. Power Management (LMR51430 buck, XC6220 LDO)
4. Safety Interlock (OCP, OVP, thermal, watchdog, logic gates)
5. Sensing (CT current, MAX31865 RTD, ADUM1250 isolator)
6. MCU (ESP32-S3, connectors, programming header)
7. User Interface (buttons, encoder, display connector)

**Custom Symbols to Import:**
- components/IKW40N120H3/IKW40N120H3.kicad_sym
- components/UCC21550/UCC21550.kicad_sym
- components/ADUM1250/ADUM1250.kicad_sym
- components/ESP32-S3/ESP32-S3-WROOM-1.kicad_sym
- components/XC6220/XC6220.kicad_sym
- components/ucc14240-q1/UCC14240-Q1.kicad_sym
- max31865/MAX31865.kicad_sym

**Reference Documents:**
- BOM.md (component part numbers)
- SAFETY_INTERLOCK_DESIGN.md (safety logic)
- VOLTAGE_DOUBLER_DESIGN.md (power stage)
- SPLIT_RAIL_BOOTSTRAP_DESIGN.md (gate driver power)
- CT_SENSING_DESIGN.md (current measurement)

title: Induction Cooker Development
description: Development of a production-grade, high-precision induction heating system based on the Simulation-First methodology. Includes Phases I-VI.

title: Lesson 01: Setup Simulation Environment
description: Establish version-controlled KiCad 8.0 project with hierarchical sheets (Power_Input, Resonant_Tank, Aux_Power, MCU_Control, Sensing_UI). Configure ngspice integration.

title: Lesson 02: Validate SPICE Fundamentals
description: Correlate simple R-LED network simulation with theory. Validate netlist-to-schematic workflow.

title: Lesson 03: Gate Charge & Miller Plateau
description: Simulate capacitive loading of MOSFET/IGBT gate. Compare weak (MCU) vs strong gate drive. Validate rise times (target ~30ns for strong drive).

title: Lesson 04: IKW40N120H3 Characterization
description: Create/Validate SPICE model for IKW40N120H3. Perform Double Pulse Test. Measure Eoff (target 0.9mJ), Tail current (~200ns), and Vce overshoot.

title: Lesson 05: UCC21550 Gate Driver Characterization
description: Simulate UCC21550 propagation delay, isolation, and dead-time. Verify dead time > 300ns to prevent shoot-through. Test CMTI immunity.

title: Lesson 06: LMR51430 Buck Converter Design
description: Simulate 24V->5V aux supply. Validate transient response (load step 100mA->600mA). Components: L=10uH, C=22uF. Verify Vout > 4.75V during dip.

title: Lesson 07: Low-Noise 3.3V LDO Design
description: Design 3.3V rail for ADC. Pi-Filter (L=1uH, C=10uF) + LDO. Target >60dB attenuation at 1.1MHz.

title: Lesson 08: UCC14140-Q1 Isolated Supply
description: Simulate isolated supply for high-side gate driver. VDD-VEE=22V, COM-VEE=4V. Verify isolation capacitance <3.5pF and CMTI >150kV/us.

title: Lesson 09: AC Input Stage Design
description: Design full-bridge rectifier and soft-start circuit (NTC/Resistor + Relay). Limit inrush < 20A. Include input filter and protection.

title: Create grounding and EMI strategy document
description: Define grounding architecture for mixed high-power/low-signal system. Document: earth ground connection, star grounding for analog sensing, power ground vs signal ground separation, isolation barrier ground treatment, shield termination strategy. Address EMI filtering requirements per IEC 60335-2-6. Create grounding diagram showing all ground domains and connections.

title: temper-placer: JAX-based PCB placement optimizer
description: Design and implement a standalone, modular PCB placement optimizer using JAX gradient-based optimization. The tool encodes expert PCB layout knowledge into differentiable loss functions for the Temper induction cooker board (~100 components). Key features: Gumbel-Softmax discrete rotation, routability-aware placement, multi-phase curriculum learning, live visualization. See TEMPER_PLACER_DESIGN.md for full specification.

title: Phase 0: Project Setup and Infrastructure
description: Set up the temper-placer project structure, dependencies, and development infrastructure. This phase establishes the foundation before any algorithm implementation.

title: Initialize Python project with pyproject.toml
description: Create temper-placer/ directory structure and pyproject.toml with dependencies: jax>=0.4.20, jaxlib>=0.4.20, optax>=0.1.7, kiutils>=1.4.0, numpy>=1.24.0, pyyaml>=6.0, plotly>=5.18.0, websockets>=12.0, click>=8.1.0, rich>=13.0.0. Include dev dependencies for pytest, ruff, mypy. Set up src/temper_placer/ package structure.

title: Set up JAX environment and verify GPU/CPU backend
description: Verify JAX installation works correctly. Test both CPU and GPU backends if available. Document environment setup in README. Create simple JAX test to verify autodiff works (e.g., gradient of quadratic function).

title: Create module directory structure
description: Create the full module structure per TEMPER_PLACER_DESIGN.md: core/, io/, geometry/, losses/, optimizer/, visualization/, and cli.py. Add __init__.py files with appropriate exports. Create placeholder modules with docstrings describing purpose.

title: Set up pytest infrastructure and CI basics
description: Configure pytest with coverage reporting. Create tests/ directory structure mirroring src/. Add conftest.py with common fixtures. Create simple smoke test that imports all modules. Consider GitHub Actions workflow for future CI.

title: Phase 1: Core Data Structures
description: Implement the fundamental data structures that all other modules depend on: PlacementState, Component, Pin, Net, Netlist, Board, Zone. These must be JAX-compatible (use jax.Array for positions) and serializable.

title: Implement PlacementState dataclass
description: Create core/state.py with PlacementState dataclass. Fields: positions (N,2) jax.Array for component centers, rotation_logits (N,4) jax.Array for Gumbel-Softmax rotation parameters. Include methods: get_rotations(temperature, key) for sampling, to_discrete() for final output, from_positions() factory method. Must work with JAX transformations (jit, grad, vmap).

title: Implement Component and Pin dataclasses
description: Create core/netlist.py with Component dataclass: ref (str), footprint (str), bounds (width, height tuple), pins (List[Pin]), net_class (str), zone (str), fixed (bool). Pin dataclass: name (str), number (str), position (tuple relative to component center), net (str). Include validation and helper methods for getting pin absolute positions given component position and rotation.

title: Implement Net and Netlist dataclasses
description: Add to core/netlist.py: Net dataclass with name (str), pins (List[Tuple[component_ref, pin_name]]), net_class (str), weight (float for wirelength importance). Netlist dataclass containing components (List[Component]) and nets (List[Net]). Include methods: get_component_index(ref), get_net_pins(net_name), get_component_nets(ref), validate() to check consistency.

title: Implement Board and Zone dataclasses
description: Create core/board.py with Board dataclass: width (float), height (float), origin (tuple), corner_radius (float), mounting_holes (List[MountingHole]), layer_stackup (LayerStackup). Zone dataclass: name (str), bounds (polygon or rectangle), components (List[str refs]). LayerStackup with methods for routable_layers(net_class), tracks_per_cell(grid_size). Include board boundary checking methods.

title: Implement ground domain model
description: Add to core/board.py: GroundDomain dataclass with name (str), bounds (polygon), star_point (tuple). GroundDomains container with methods: get_domain_at_point(position), path_crosses_boundary(p1, p2), get_valid_crossing_point(). This models PGND/CGND/ISOGND splits for the Temper board and is critical for ground crossing loss.

title: Write unit tests for core data structures
description: Create tests/test_core.py with comprehensive tests: PlacementState creation and JAX compatibility, Component/Pin position calculations with rotation, Netlist validation and querying, Board boundary checks, Zone containment tests, Ground domain crossing detection. Use pytest fixtures for common test data.

title: Phase 2: IO Layer - KiCad and Config Parsing
description: Implement input/output operations: parsing KiCad files via kiutils, reading YAML constraint configs, loading footprint dimensions, and exporting placements back to KiCad format. This phase enables the tool to work with real PCB data.

title: Implement KiCad PCB parser using kiutils
description: Create io/kicad_parser.py to read .kicad_pcb files via kiutils. Extract: component positions/rotations, footprint references, net connections, board outline. Convert to internal Netlist representation. Handle hierarchical designs (sheet instances). Parse net classes from board design rules. Must handle both placed and unplaced components.

title: Implement KiCad schematic parser for netlist extraction
description: Create io/kicad_sch_parser.py to extract netlist from .kicad_sch files. Parse hierarchical schematics (temper.kicad_sch references sub-sheets). Extract component references, pin connections, net names. Build Net objects from wire/label connections. Handle hierarchical labels for inter-sheet nets. Alternative: use KiCad's netlist export and parse that.

title: Implement footprint dimension loader
description: Create io/footprints.py to extract component bounding boxes and pin positions from footprints. Options: 1) Parse .kicad_mod files directly, 2) Use kiutils footprint parsing, 3) Build lookup table from common packages. For Temper: handle TO-247 (IGBTs), SOIC-16, SOT-23-6, ESP32-S3-WROOM module, 0603/0805/1206 passives. Return (width, height) bounds and pin relative positions.

title: Implement YAML constraint config parser
description: Create io/constraints.py to parse YAML config files per TEMPER_PLACER_DESIGN.md Section 6. Parse: board.yaml (dimensions, stackup), zones.yaml (zone definitions, component assignments), clearances.yaml (net class rules), thermal.yaml (edge constraints), loops.yaml (critical loop definitions), special_zones.yaml (antenna keepout, fixed placements). Validate config consistency.

title: Implement KiCad PCB writer for placement export
description: Create io/kicad_writer.py to write optimized placements back to .kicad_pcb. Load template PCB, update component positions and rotations, preserve all other data (traces, zones, text). Validate output opens correctly in KiCad. Handle rotation mapping (0/90/180/270 degrees). Preserve component properties and attributes.

title: Create Temper-specific config files
description: Create configs/temper/ directory with YAML configs for the Temper board per TEMPER_PLACER_DESIGN.md Section 6: board.yaml (100x150mm, 4-layer stackup), zones.yaml (HIGH_VOLTAGE, HALF_BRIDGE, POWER_MANAGEMENT, CONTROL, SENSING zones with component assignments), clearances.yaml (HV 10mm, Power 6mm rules), thermal.yaml (Q1/Q2 edge constraints), loops.yaml (gate drive, bootstrap loops).

title: Write integration tests for IO layer
description: Create tests/test_io.py with tests: parse actual Temper .kicad_pcb and .kicad_sch files, verify component count matches BOM (~100), verify net extraction, round-trip test (parse → modify → write → parse), validate constraint config loading. Create test fixtures with minimal KiCad files.

title: Phase 3: Geometry Engine
description: Implement differentiable geometry primitives in JAX: points, rectangles, polygons, signed distance functions (SDF), overlap detection, rotation transforms. These form the mathematical foundation for all loss functions and must support JAX autodiff.

title: Implement basic geometric primitives
description: Create geometry/primitives.py with JAX-compatible functions: point operations (distance, midpoint), rectangle representation (center, width, height), axis-aligned bounding box (AABB) operations, polygon representation (list of vertices). All functions must be compatible with jax.jit and jax.grad. Use jax.numpy throughout.

title: Implement rotation transforms for discrete angles
description: Create geometry/transform.py with rotation functions for 0°, 90°, 180°, 270°. Functions: rotate_point(point, rotation_onehot, center), rotate_rectangle(rect, rotation_onehot), get_rotated_bounds(width, height, rotation_onehot) - note 90/270 swap width/height. Transform pin positions based on component rotation. Must be differentiable through Gumbel-Softmax rotation selection.

title: Implement signed distance functions (SDF)
description: Create geometry/sdf.py with differentiable SDF functions: sdf_rectangle(point, center, width, height) returns signed distance (negative inside), sdf_circle(point, center, radius), sdf_polygon(point, vertices). SDF enables smooth overlap detection - overlap = relu(-sdf). Include sdf_union and sdf_intersection for combining shapes.

title: Implement component overlap detection
description: Create geometry/overlap.py with functions: box_box_distance(pos1, rot1, bounds1, pos2, rot2, bounds2) returns minimum distance between two rotated boxes (negative if overlapping), component_overlap_amount(same params) returns overlap area estimate for severe overlaps. Use SDF-based approach for differentiability. Handle axis-aligned approximation for rotated boxes.

title: Implement smooth min/max functions (LogSumExp)
description: Create geometry/smooth.py with smooth approximations: smooth_max(x, alpha) using LogSumExp, smooth_min(x, alpha), smooth_relu(x, beta) for softer transitions. These are critical for differentiable HPWL computation. Include parameter alpha/beta that can be annealed during training (high=sharper approximation). Document gradient behavior.

title: Implement polygon area calculation (shoelace)
description: Create geometry/polygon.py with polygon_area(vertices) using shoelace formula. This is differentiable and used for gate drive loop area loss. Also implement: polygon_centroid(vertices), point_in_polygon(point, vertices) for zone containment, polygon_intersection_area(poly1, poly2) if needed for overlap.

title: Implement distance to board edge functions
description: Add to geometry/primitives.py: distance_to_edge(point, board) returns distance to nearest board boundary, distance_to_specific_edge(point, edge, board) for TOP/BOTTOM/LEFT/RIGHT. Used by thermal loss to constrain IGBTs near edges. Handle board corner radius in distance calculation.

title: Write unit tests for geometry engine
description: Create tests/test_geometry.py with tests: rotation correctness at 0/90/180/270, SDF sign (negative inside, positive outside, zero on boundary), overlap detection for overlapping and non-overlapping boxes, smooth min/max approximation accuracy, polygon area for known shapes (square, triangle). Test JAX grad compatibility for all functions.

title: Phase 5: Validation-in-the-Loop (DRC + ngspice)
description: Implement integration with KiCad DRC and ngspice for real design rule and electrical validation during optimization. This ensures placements are not just geometrically valid but electrically sound. Non-differentiable validation is run periodically and contributes penalty terms to the loss function.

title: Implement KiCad DRC validator wrapper
description: Create validation/drc.py with KiCadDRCValidator class. Use 'kicad-cli pcb drc' (KiCad 7+) to run headless DRC. Parse JSON output to extract violations (severity, rule type, position, affected items). Include DRCViolation dataclass. Handle error cases (KiCad not installed, invalid PCB). Test with actual Temper PCB file.

title: Implement validation schedule configuration
description: Create validation/scheduler.py and configs/temper/validation.yaml. Configure: DRC interval (every N epochs), SPICE interval, penalty weights, which simulations to run, fail-on-error behavior. Support 'final phase' with increased validation frequency. Parse YAML config and expose to optimizer.

title: Integrate validation into training loop
description: Modify optimizer/train.py to call validation at scheduled intervals. Export current placement to temp PCB file for DRC. Convert state to positions dict for SPICE. Add validation penalties to loss (non-differentiable). Log validation results and violations. Handle validation failures gracefully (continue vs abort).

title: Write integration tests for validation pipeline
description: Create tests/test_validation.py. Test: DRC runs on valid/invalid PCBs, SPICE simulations execute and parse correctly, penalty computation matches expected values, caching works (subsequent calls within interval return cached value), validation integrates with training loop. Use Temper PCB and existing SPICE testbenches as fixtures.

title: Implement DRC penalty computation
description: Add compute_drc_penalty() method to KiCadDRCValidator. Weight errors heavily (10.0), warnings lightly (1.0). Consider violation type: clearance violations critical for HV, silk overlap warnings minor. Return scalar penalty value for loss function. Include violation categorization by severity and type.

title: Implement DRCLoss with caching for periodic evaluation
description: Create losses/drc_loss.py with DRCLoss class. Only run actual DRC every N epochs (configurable interval) due to overhead. Cache last penalty value between runs. Track violation history for visualization. Integrate with loss aggregator. Handle temp file creation/cleanup for PCB export during validation.

title: Implement ngspice simulation wrapper
description: Create validation/spice.py with NgspiceValidator class. Run ngspice in batch mode (-b flag). Parse output for measurements. Handle SPICE template files with parameter substitution (e.g., {{LOOP_INDUCTANCE}}). Extract key metrics from .measure statements. Test with existing Temper simulation testbenches.

title: Implement loop inductance estimation from placement
description: Add estimate_loop_inductance() to NgspiceValidator. Calculate loop area from component positions using shoelace formula. Use simplified inductance model: L ≈ μ₀ * Area / h (trace height). For Temper: gate drive loops (U_GD to Q1/Q2), bootstrap loop (D_BOOT, C_BOOT, U_GD). Return inductance in Henries for SPICE models.

title: Create SPICE templates for placement-dependent simulation
description: Create validation/spice_templates/ directory with parameterized SPICE netlists: gate_drive.cir (gate drive loop with placement-dependent inductance), bootstrap_charging.cir (verify V_BOOT reaches 12V+), power_integrity.cir (DC bus voltage drop). Use {{PARAM}} syntax for substitution. Base on existing simulation/testbenches/ files.

title: Implement gate drive simulation for EMI validation
description: Add run_gate_drive_simulation() to NgspiceValidator. Extract gate loop inductance from placement. Substitute into SPICE template. Run transient simulation of switching. Extract metrics: peak di/dt, ringing voltage, settling time. Flag placements that cause excessive ringing (>20% overshoot). This validates EMI-critical placement constraints.

title: Implement bootstrap charging simulation
description: Add run_bootstrap_simulation() to NgspiceValidator. Verify bootstrap capacitor charges to sufficient voltage (>12V for UCC21550). Extract V_BOOT final value. Consider bootstrap loop inductance from placement. Flag placements where charging is marginal or fails. Critical for high-side gate driver operation.

title: Implement SPICE penalty computation
description: Add compute_spice_penalty() to NgspiceValidator. Heavy penalty (50.0) for failed simulations. Graduated penalty for marginal results (e.g., V_BOOT between 12-14V). Aggregate penalties across multiple simulation types. Return scalar for loss function integration. Include detailed breakdown for debugging.

title: Phase 4: Loss Functions
description: Implement all differentiable loss functions: wirelength (HPWL), overlap, boundary, HV clearance, zone membership, ground crossing, thermal edge, loop area, congestion. Each loss function must be JAX-compatible and include weight scheduling for curriculum learning.

title: Implement LossFunction base class and interface
description: Create losses/base.py with abstract LossFunction class. Define interface: name property, __call__(positions, rotations, context) -> float, weight_schedule(epoch, total_epochs) -> float for curriculum learning. Create CompositeLoss class that aggregates multiple losses with weights and returns breakdown dict. Ensure JAX compatibility.

title: Implement congestion loss
description: Create losses/congestion.py with CongestionLoss class. Estimate routing difficulty by dividing board into grid cells. For each net, estimate cells traversed (bounding box heuristic). Sum demand per cell, compare to supply (routable layers × tracks per mm). Penalize overflow. Consider layer stackup: L1/L4 routable, L2/L3 planes.

title: Implement regularization losses (spread, entropy)
description: Create losses/regularization.py with SpreadLoss (encourage components to spread out, prevent clustering) and RotationEntropyLoss (encourage exploration of rotations early in training). Both should anneal down as training progresses. Spread uses negative log of pairwise distances. Entropy uses -sum(p*log(p)) for rotation probabilities.

title: Implement weight scheduling for curriculum learning
description: Create losses/scheduler.py with weight schedule functions. Define get_weights_for_epoch(epoch, total_epochs) that returns LossWeights with curriculum learning: Phase 1 (spread), Phase 2 (feasibility), Phase 3 (design rules), Phase 4 (performance), Phase 5 (refinement). Use smooth_step function for gradual transitions. Configure via training.yaml.

title: Write unit tests for all loss functions
description: Create tests/test_losses.py with tests for each loss function. Test: correct output for known inputs, gradient computation via JAX, boundary cases (zero loss for valid placements, positive for violations), weight scheduling produces expected curves. Create test fixtures with simple netlists and placements.

title: Implement wirelength loss (HPWL)
description: Create losses/wirelength.py with WirelengthLoss class. Compute Half-Perimeter Wire Length using smooth LogSumExp approximation of min/max (parameter alpha, annealed during training). Sum HPWL across all nets, optionally weighted by net importance. Handle nets with <2 pins. Must be fully differentiable for JAX grad.

title: Implement overlap loss
description: Create losses/overlap.py with OverlapLoss class. Compute overlap penalty using SDF-based box-box distance from geometry module. For each component pair, penalize if distance < 0 (overlapping). Use squared penalty: relu(-dist)^2. Handle rotation via bounds transformation. Weight: high (100.0) - hard constraint.

title: Implement boundary loss
description: Create losses/boundary.py with BoundaryLoss class. Penalize components outside board boundaries. For each component, compute distance to each edge accounting for component bounds. Penalize if any part extends beyond board. Also penalize components in mounting hole keepout zones. Weight: high (100.0) - hard constraint.

title: Implement HV clearance loss
description: Create losses/clearance.py with ClearanceLoss class. Enforce minimum distance between high-voltage and low-voltage components per PCB_SPECIFICATION.md (10mm reinforced isolation). Identify HV components from net class assignments. Compute pairwise distances, penalize violations. Support multiple clearance rules (HV-LV: 10mm, HV-Power: 6mm).

title: Implement zone membership loss
description: Create losses/zone.py with ZoneLoss class. Penalize components outside their assigned zones. For each component, compute distance to zone boundary (positive if outside). Use squared penalty for violations. Support rectangular and polygonal zones. Reference zone assignments from constraints config.

title: Implement ground crossing loss
description: Create losses/ground_crossing.py with GroundCrossingLoss class. Penalize nets that span multiple ground domains without going through star ground point. For each net, identify ground domains touched by pins. If multiple domains, check if path can route through star point. Heavy penalty for impossible crossings. Critical for return current integrity.

title: Implement thermal loss
description: Create losses/thermal.py with ThermalLoss class. Penalize thermal components (IGBTs) that are far from designated board edge. Per thermal.yaml config: Q1/Q2 must be within 5mm of TOP edge. Also penalize hot components too close to temperature-sensitive parts (ESP32, RTD ICs). Support edge specification (TOP/BOTTOM/LEFT/RIGHT).

title: Implement loop area loss
description: Create losses/loop_area.py with LoopAreaLoss class. Minimize area of critical current loops defined in loops.yaml. For each loop, get pin positions forming the loop, compute polygon area via shoelace formula. Penalize area exceeding max_area threshold. Critical loops: gate_drive_high, gate_drive_low, bootstrap, buck_switch.

title: Phase 6: Optimizer Core
description: Implement the optimization engine: Gumbel-Softmax for discrete rotation, optax-based gradient descent with Adam, multi-phase training with curriculum learning, temperature annealing, post-processing (grid snap, discrete rotation refinement).

title: Implement Gumbel-Softmax for discrete rotation
description: Create optimizer/gumbel.py with gumbel_softmax(logits, key, temperature) function. Implement: Gumbel noise sampling, softmax with temperature, straight-through estimator (soft forward, hard backward). Support temperature annealing schedule (5.0 → 0.1). Add sample_rotation() that returns one-hot rotation vectors. Test gradient flow.

title: Write unit tests for optimizer
description: Create tests/test_optimizer.py with tests: Gumbel-Softmax produces valid one-hot (sum=1), temperature annealing produces expected schedule, single training step reduces loss on simple problem, gradient clipping activates on large gradients, checkpointing round-trips correctly. Use simple 5-component test problem.

title: Implement optimizer configuration dataclasses
description: Create optimizer/config.py with dataclasses: OptimizerConfig (learning_rate, warmup_steps, decay_type, gradient_clip), TrainingConfig (total_epochs, phases with epoch counts and weights), ConvergenceCriteria (min_improvement, patience, max violations). Support loading from training.yaml. Include defaults for Temper project.

title: Implement learning rate scheduler
description: Create optimizer/scheduler.py with learning rate schedules using optax: warmup_cosine_decay_schedule, constant_schedule, exponential_decay. Support configuration from TrainingConfig. Return optax schedule object. Include get_temperature(epoch, total_epochs) for Gumbel-Softmax annealing (exponential decay from initial to final).

title: Implement core training loop
description: Create optimizer/train.py with train() function. Initialize: positions (from initial placement), rotation_logits (uniform), Adam optimizer state. Per iteration: sample rotations via Gumbel-Softmax, compute composite loss, compute gradients via jax.grad, apply updates via optax. Return final PlacementState. Support early stopping on convergence.

title: Implement multi-phase training

title: Epic: Bootstrap Supply Safety for Control Freak Clone
description:
Address critical bootstrap supply risks for high-precision, low-temperature induction control (Breville Control Freak clone standard).

CONTEXT:
The initial bootstrap vs UCC14140 decision (temper-urx.1) was correct for cost/simplicity, but did not adequately address two critical risks for high-end induction control:

RISK 1: Low-Temperature Burst Mode Bootstrap Depletion
- Control Freak holds liquids at 30°C (86°F) using burst mode (switch ON for cycles, then sleep for 100ms-seconds)
- During sleep periods, C_BOOT leaks charge
- If sleep time too long: VDDA drops below UVLO → high-side driver fails → massive IGBT heating
- Simple bootstrap: No refresh during sleep
- UCC14140: Would not have this problem (always powered)

RISK 2: Miller Effect Shoot-Through Without Negative Bias
- Half-bridge dV/dt: 300V in 50ns = 6V/ns at switch node
- Miller current through CGD (1.5nF): I = 1.5nF × 6V/ns = 9mA
- With 10kΩ pull-down: V_rise = 90V >> VGE(th) = 5V → FALSE TURN-ON
- Without negative bias (-5V): High-side can turn on during low-side ON → SHOOT-THROUGH → catastrophic failure
- UCC21550 has NO Active Miller Clamp (unlike UCC21520/UCC21750)

CURRENT HARDWARE SELECTION ISSUES:
- UCC21550ADWR: UVLO ~5V (too low for SiC - needs 15-20V to fully turn on)
- Simple bootstrap (0V to +15V): No negative bias, vulnerable to Miller turn-on
- Missing: Active Miller Clamp or negative rail generation

REQUIRED SOLUTION: 'Robust Bootstrap' Architecture
- Cost target: ~.50 (vs  simple,  isolated)
- Split-rail bootstrap: +15V / -5V gate drive from single bootstrap
- Higher UVLO protection: UCC21550BDWR variant (8-9V threshold)
- Burst mode management: Firmware refresh pulses + capacitor sizing
- Miller protection: Zener/cap network creates virtual negative rail

SUBTASKS TO CREATE:
1. Analyze burst mode bootstrap depletion (sleep time vs capacitor droop)
2. Calculate Miller current and validate need for negative bias
3. Design split-rail bootstrap circuit (Zener/cap network for -5V)
4. Evaluate UCC21550 A vs B variant UVLO for SiC gate drive
5. Implement firmware refresh pulse strategy for burst mode
6. Verify complete robust bootstrap circuit in simulation
7. Update GATE_DRIVER_POWER_ARCHITECTURE_DECISION.md with high-precision requirements

title: Analyze burst mode bootstrap capacitor depletion
description:
Quantify bootstrap capacitor voltage droop during Control Freak low-temperature burst mode operation.

OBJECTIVE:
Determine maximum safe sleep time before C_BOOT voltage drops below UVLO threshold, requiring firmware refresh pulses.

ANALYSIS REQUIREMENTS:
1. Bootstrap capacitor discharge mechanisms:
   - Gate charge delivery: Q_total = Q_G × N_pulses
   - Quiescent current drain: I_Q × t_sleep (UCC21550 datasheet)
   - Leakage current: I_leak × t_sleep (capacitor ESR, PCB leakage)
   
2. Voltage droop calculation:
   - Initial: V_BOOT = 15V (fully charged)
   - After N pulses: V_BOOT = 15V - (Q_total / C_BOOT)
   - UVLO threshold: 
     * UCC21550A: ~5V (inadequate for SiC)
     * UCC21550B: ~8-9V (recommended minimum)
   
3. Burst mode scenarios:
   - Low temp (30°C): 10 cycles ON, 1000ms sleep (example)
   - Medium temp (60°C): 50 cycles ON, 500ms sleep
   - High temp (95°C): Continuous switching (no burst mode)
   
4. Calculate for IKW40N120H3 IGBT:
   - Q_G = 240nC per pulse
   - Switching frequency: 50kHz during burst
   - Example: 10 pulses = 10 × 240nC = 2.4µC charge transfer
   - With C_BOOT = 1µF: ΔV = 2.4µC / 1µF = 2.4V droop
   
5. Add safety margin:
   - Worst case: 15V - 2.4V - margin = 12.6V (still above UVLO B variant)
   - But: Add quiescent + leakage for 1000ms sleep period
   
6. Determine:
   - Maximum sleep time before UVLO trip (A variant vs B variant)
   - Required C_BOOT size for 2-second sleep safety margin
   - Firmware refresh pulse interval recommendation

DELIVERABLE:
- Spreadsheet or calculation document with:
  * Sleep time vs voltage droop curves
  * Safety margin analysis for different C_BOOT sizes (1µF, 2.2µF, 4.7µF)
  * Recommendation: C_BOOT size + firmware refresh interval
  * Comparison: A variant (5V UVLO) vs B variant (8-9V UVLO) safety

REFERENCES:
- UCC21550 datasheet: Quiescent current specs
- IKW40N120H3 datasheet: Gate charge curves (components/IKW40N120H3/)
- GATE_DRIVER_POWER_ARCHITECTURE_DECISION.md

title: Calculate Miller current and validate negative bias requirement
description:
Quantify Miller capacitance injection current and validate need for negative gate bias to prevent shoot-through.

OBJECTIVE:
Prove mathematically that simple bootstrap (0V to +15V) is inadequate for 300V, 6V/ns dV/dt half-bridge switching.

ANALYSIS REQUIREMENTS:

1. Miller Current Calculation:
   - Switch node dV/dt: 300V / t_rise
     * Fast switching (SiC): t_rise = 20ns → dV/dt = 15V/ns
     * Medium (IGBT): t_rise = 50ns → dV/dt = 6V/ns
     * Slow (conservative): t_rise = 100ns → dV/dt = 3V/ns
   
   - Miller capacitance (IKW40N120H3): C_GD = 1.5nF (@ V_CE = 0V, higher at high voltage)
   
   - Injection current: I_Miller = C_GD × dV/dt
     * Fast: I = 1.5nF × 15V/ns = 22.5mA
     * Medium: I = 1.5nF × 6V/ns = 9mA
     * Slow: I = 1.5nF × 3V/ns = 4.5mA

2. Gate Voltage Rise Analysis:

   Option A: 10kΩ pull-down (current design):
   - V_rise = I_Miller × R_GS = 9mA × 10kΩ = 90V (!)
   - VGE(th) = 5V typical, 6V max
   - **FAILURE:** 90V >> 6V → guaranteed false turn-on
   
   Option B: 1kΩ pull-down (stronger):
   - V_rise = 9mA × 1kΩ = 9V
   - **STILL FAILS:** 9V > 6V → likely false turn-on
   
   Option C: Negative bias (-5V):
   - Starting point: V_GE = -5V
   - After Miller injection: V_GE = -5V + (I × R_GS × t_pulse)
   - Transient pulse: t_pulse ~ 10-50ns (edge duration)
   - Even with 10kΩ: Needs 120mA to cross 0V threshold
   - **SAFE:** -5V provides adequate margin

3. Active Miller Clamp Alternative:
   - UCC21520/UCC21750 have dedicated CLAMP pin
   - Creates low-impedance path (~10Ω) during off-state
   - V_rise = 9mA × 10Ω = 0.09V (negligible)
   - **Would work without negative bias**
   - BUT: UCC21550 does NOT have this feature

4. Energy Dissipation in Pull-Down:
   - E = 0.5 × C_GD × (ΔV)²
   - E = 0.5 × 1.5nF × (300V)² = 67.5µJ per edge
   - At 50kHz: P = 67.5µJ × 50kHz × 2 edges = 6.75mW (manageable)

5. Shoot-Through Risk Assessment:
   - Without negative bias: High-side gate can reach VGE(th) during low-side turn-on
   - Shoot-through duration: Even 50ns overlap at 40A → catastrophic
   - Insurance: Negative bias keeps gate firmly below threshold

DELIVERABLE:
- Calculation document with:
  * Miller current vs dV/dt curves for different rise times
  * Gate voltage transient plots for different R_GS values
  * Safety margin analysis: 0V vs -5V vs -8V bias
  * Recommendation: Required negative bias voltage for 2× safety margin
  * Comparison: Active Miller Clamp IC vs split-rail bootstrap cost/complexity

REFERENCES:
- IKW40N120H3 datasheet: C_GD vs V_CE curves, V_GE(th) statistics
- UCC21550 datasheet: Output impedance during off-state
- IEEE papers on Miller effect in half-bridge converters

title: Design split-rail bootstrap circuit with Zener negative bias
description:
Design the 'Robust Bootstrap' circuit that generates +15V / -5V split-rail gate drive from a single bootstrap supply.

OBJECTIVE:
Create schematic and component selection for split-capacitor bootstrap that provides negative bias without isolated DC-DC converter.

CIRCUIT TOPOLOGY: Split-Rail Bootstrap

Standard bootstrap (current design):
  VDD_15V → D_BOOT → C_BOOT → SW_NODE
  Provides: V_GS = 0V (OFF) to +15V (ON)
  Cost: ~

Robust bootstrap (target design):
  VDD_15V → D_BOOT → C_BOOT_POS → [Zener Network] → C_BOOT_NEG → SW_NODE
  Provides: V_GS = -5V (OFF) to +15V (ON)
  Cost: ~.50

DESIGN REQUIREMENTS:

1. Zener/Capacitor Network Options:

   Option A: Series Zener (Simple):
   
   - Zener creates voltage shift: Driver VSS sits at SW_NODE + 5V
   - When driver outputs LOW (0V relative to its VSS), gate sees -5V relative to SW_NODE
   - Cost: +1 Zener (/bin/zsh.20), +0 extra caps
   
   Option B: Dual-Cap Split:
   
   - Creates true split rail
   - More stable voltage division
   - Cost: +1 Zener, +1 cap (/bin/zsh.50 total)

2. Component Selection:

   Zener Diode:
   - Voltage: 5.1V (standard E12 value, creates -5V bias)
   - Power: 500mW minimum (1W safer)
   - Package: SOD-123 or DO-214
   - Example: BZX84C5V1 (5.1V, 350mW, SOT-23) or 1N4733A (5.1V, 1W, DO-41)
   - Consider: Temperature coefficient (±5%/°C for std Zener)
   
   Bootstrap Capacitors:
   - C_BOOT_POS: 2.2µF → 4.7µF (increased from 1µF for burst mode margin)
   - C_BOOT_NEG: 1µF (holds negative rail)
   - Voltage rating: 50V (15V nominal + margin)
   - Dielectric: X7R or X5R (not Y5V - too much voltage coefficient)
   - Package: 1206 or 1210
   
   Bootstrap Diode (no change):
   - D_BOOT: C4D10120A (SiC Schottky, 1200V, 10A)
   - Why SiC: Zero reverse recovery charge (critical for EMI)

3. Voltage Budget Analysis:
   - VDD source: 15V
   - Diode drop (SiC): 0.4V @ 100mA
   - C_BOOT_POS charges to: 15V - 0.4V = 14.6V
   - Zener drop: 5.1V (typ), 5.3V (max @ 25°C)
   - Driver VDD-VSS span: 14.6V
   - Gate drive range: -5.1V (OFF) to +9.5V (ON)
   
   **PROBLEM:** +9.5V may be insufficient for full IGBT saturation!
   
4. Revised Topology to Maintain +15V ON:
   
   Use **Schottky + Zener Clamp** instead:
   
   - During ON: Current flows through Schottky (0.3V drop), full 14.7V available
   - During OFF: Zener clamps gate at -5V below SW_NODE
   - Maintains full +15V drive capability

5. Firmware Refresh Pulse Integration:
   - Add low-side pre-charge pulse before each burst sequence
   - Duration: 100µs minimum (10× longer than bootstrap time constant)
   - Frequency: Before every burst, or every 500ms during sleep (whichever shorter)

DELIVERABLE:
- Full schematic of split-rail bootstrap circuit
- Component BOM with part numbers and costs
- SPICE netlist snippet for integration into sim_16
- PCB layout guidelines (Zener placement, trace routing)
- Voltage budget spreadsheet (worst-case analysis)
- Comparison table: Simple vs Robust bootstrap (cost, performance, safety margin)

VALIDATION:
- Will be verified in temper-8l2.6 (simulation)

REFERENCES:
- Application Note AN-6005 'Bootstrap Circuit Design' (International Rectifier)
- Texas Instruments SLUA618 'Gate Drive Optimization'
- Zener voltage vs temperature characteristics

title: Evaluate UCC21550A vs B variant for SiC gate drive requirements
description:
Compare UCC21550 variants and recommend correct UVLO threshold for safe SiC/IGBT gate drive.

OBJECTIVE: Determine whether UCC21550ADWR or UCC21550BDWR is appropriate for IKW40N120H3 IGBT with robust bootstrap supply.

VARIANT COMPARISON:

UCC21550A (current selection):
- UVLO rising: 6.0V (VDD)
- UVLO falling: 5.7V (hysteresis)
- Min operating voltage: 6.5V (datasheet)

UCC21550B:
- UVLO rising: 8.5V (VDD)  
- UVLO falling: 7.9V
- Min operating voltage: 9.2V

UCC21550C:
- UVLO rising: 12.5V
- UVLO falling: 11.5V  
- Min operating voltage: 13.5V

ANALYSIS REQUIREMENTS:

1. IGBT Gate Drive Requirements (IKW40N120H3):
- VGE(th): 4.5V min, 5.0V typ, 6.0V max (threshold)
- Recommended VGE: 15V (full saturation)
- Absolute minimum for safe operation: 10V (prevents resistive heating)
- Absolute maximum: +20V / -20V

2. Risk Assessment for Variant A (6V UVLO):
- Scenario: Bootstrap droops to 7V during long burst sleep
- Variant A behavior: Still switches (7V > 6V UVLO)
- IGBT behavior at VGE=7V: Partially on, high RDS, overheating
- VERDICT: UNSAFE for reliability

3. Risk Assessment for Variant B (8.5V UVLO):
- Scenario: Bootstrap droops to 8V during burst sleep
- Variant B behavior: UVLO triggered, driver disabled
- IGBT behavior: Safely off, no switching
- VERDICT: SAFE - fail-safe protection

4. Risk Assessment for Variant C (12.5V UVLO):  
- Very conservative
- May trigger prematurely during normal operation
- VERDICT: Overly cautious for this application

5. Burst Mode Droop Analysis Integration:
- Link to temper-8l2.1 results
- Determine: Will robust bootstrap (2.2uF) + refresh pulses keep voltage above 8.5V?
- If yes: Variant B provides fail-safe without false triggers
- If no: Need larger capacitor or more frequent refresh

6. Temperature Effects:
- UVLO threshold shifts: Typically +/-5% over temp
- Worst case (hot): 8.5V × 0.95 = 8.1V
- Must ensure bootstrap stays above 8.5V worst-case

DELIVERABLE:
- Variant comparison table
- Recommendation: A vs B vs C with justification
- Update GATE_DRIVER_POWER_ARCHITECTURE_DECISION.md with variant selection
- Note any PCB changes needed (variant A and B are pin-compatible)

EXPECTED OUTCOME: Recommend UCC21550BDWR for fail-safe UVLO protection

title: Verify selected components in the induction_curriculum.md can actually work together for the intended project (which is documented in the md file). The datasheet pdfs are in the md file. Use reasonable strategies to prove these components make sense to go together.
Fix critical firmware bugs from extraction review
description: During firmware extraction from induction_curriculum.md, critical failure modes were identified across all control modules. This epic tracks fixes for 35+ bugs found in pan_detect.c, pid_control.c, pll_control.c, safety.c, and state_machine.c. Priority order: CRITICAL bugs first (safety-affecting), then HIGH severity (race conditions, uninitialized state).

title: pan_detect: detect_pan_presence() always returns PAN_ABSENT
description: CRITICAL: detect_pan_presence() has static timer=NULL that is never initialized. Function always returns PAN_ABSENT at line 141, making pan detection non-functional. Fix: Add pan_detect_set_timer() to inject timer handle, or use module-level static.

title: state_machine: No maximum preheat time limit
description: MEDIUM: STATE_PREHEAT has no timeout. If temperature sensor fails or pan has poor coupling, system could heat indefinitely. Fix: Add max preheat timeout (e.g., 10 minutes) with transition to FAULT if exceeded.

title: state_machine: Division by zero in NO_PAN impedance check
description: MEDIUM: Line 588-589 divides by initial_pan_impedance which could be 0 if pan detection had edge_count=0. Fix: Add guard for initial_pan_impedance==0.

title: pll_control: Hardcoded 1ms loop assumption in integrator
description: MEDIUM: pll_update_loop() line 109 uses *0.001f assuming 1ms loop. If called at different rate, integrator will be wrong. Fix: Pass dt_sec parameter or compute from timestamps.

title: pan_detect: Race condition on edge_count ISR variable
description: HIGH: edge_count is modified by ISR (line 43) and read non-atomically in pan_detect_run() (lines 88, 107). On 32-bit ESP32 this is usually safe but not guaranteed. Fix: Use atomic read or critical section around edge_count access.

title: pid_control: Global g_pid never initialized
description: CRITICAL: g_pid (line 22) is static but never initialized. pid_update() uses uninitialized/zero gains, causing PID output to always be 0. Fix: Add auto-initialization with default gains on first call, or require explicit init.

title: pid_control: g_last_dt hardcoded, never updated
description: HIGH: g_last_dt=0.01f (line 23) is never updated from actual loop timing. Integral and derivative terms will be wrong if control loop runs at different rate. Fix: Add pid_set_dt() function or compute dt from timestamps.

title: pll_control: pll_update() is empty stub - PLL never tracks
description: CRITICAL: pll_update() (lines 137-141) is a no-op placeholder. It never reads phase measurement or calls pll_update_loop(). PLL frequency tracking is completely non-functional. Fix: Implement phase measurement from MCPWM capture and call pll_update_loop().

title: pll_control: pll_timer never set - hardware never updated
description: CRITICAL: pll_timer (line 54) is declared but never assigned. mcpwm_timer_set_period() calls in pll_update_loop() and pll_reset() do nothing because pll_timer is always NULL. Fix: Add pll_set_timer() function to inject timer handle.

title: safety: Non-ESP builds have no safety checks
description: CRITICAL: All safety functions wrapped in #ifdef ESP_PLATFORM. Without ESP-IDF: check_hardware_interlocks() returns true, run_safety_check() returns SAFETY_OK, watchdog functions are no-ops. This means desktop testing has zero safety coverage. Fix: Add simulation stubs with configurable fault injection, or always-fault mode for testing.

title: state_machine: cooking_time_ms never decremented
description: HIGH: cooking_time_ms checked for ==0 at line 549 but never decremented anywhere in state_heating_update() or elsewhere. Timer feature is completely broken - will never trigger completion. Fix: Add timer decrement logic in state_heating_update().

title: state_machine: Blocking delay_ms() calls in state handlers
description: HIGH: delay_ms(2000) calls at lines 395, 551, 598 block the entire control loop. During these delays: no watchdog feeding, no safety checks, no user input. Fix: Convert to non-blocking state timeouts using state_duration.

title: Level 4: Resonant Tank & Load Integration

title: Add external hardware watchdog IC circuit
description:
SAFETY GAP: Current design relies on ESP32 Task Watchdog (software) for MCU lockup protection. If ESP32 has a hard lockup (ESD, silicon bug, power glitch), software WDT cannot trigger. The hardware interlocks (OCP/OVP/thermal) only detect fault conditions, not MCU failure.

SOLUTION: Add external hardware watchdog IC (e.g., TPS3823, MAX6369, TPL5010) that:
1. Requires periodic heartbeat from ESP32 GPIO
2. Has configurable timeout (1-2 seconds)
3. Has open-drain RESET output that can:
   - Assert ESP32 RESET (force reboot)
   - OR directly assert UCC21550 DISABLE (immediate power stage disable)
   - OR both via OR gate into interlock logic

RECOMMENDED PARTS:
- TPS3823-33 (TI): 1.6s timeout, active-low RESET, SOT-23-5
- MAX6369 (Maxim): Selectable timeout, open-drain, SOT-23
- TPL5010 (TI): Ultra-low power, programmable interval, good for battery backup

INTEGRATION:
- Connect WDI (watchdog input) to ESP32 GPIO with periodic toggle in control loop
- Connect RESET output to OR gate feeding UCC21550 DISABLE
- Add LED indicator for watchdog fault
- Consider adding to SAFETY_INTERLOCK_DESIGN.md fault integration logic

VERIFICATION:
- Simulate watchdog timeout behavior
- Test with intentional firmware hang
- Verify power stage disables within watchdog timeout period

title: Refine project plan
description: This project is aimed at creating a clone of a breville control freak. It's being built by a relative novice so the build is being broken down into a course. The course starts from zero and builds up to a functional clone. The project prioritizes modularity, iteration, verification, simulation, and safety.
design:
## Architecture

The project is a **simulation-first, modular induction cooking system** designed as a 44-lesson educational curriculum. It guides developers from basic component characterization through full system integration, emphasizing safety, verification, and professional engineering practices.

**System Overview:**
- **Target:** 1500-2000W high-precision induction cooker (Breville Control Freak clone)
- **Methodology:** Simulation-first (validate in SPICE before hardware)
- **Controller:** ESP32-S3 dual-core MCU
- **Power Stage:** Half-bridge resonant inverter (30-40kHz)
- **Safety:** Hardware interlocks + firmware redundancy + galvanic isolation

**Six-Phase Structure:**
1. **Phase I (Lessons 1-10):** Simulation environment & component characterization
2. **Phase II (Lessons 11-18):** Power stage simulation & resonant tank design
3. **Phase III (Lessons 19-26):** Control system & sensing simulation
4. **Phase IV (Lessons 27-32):** Firmware development & algorithm validation
5. **Phase V (Lessons 33-38):** PCB design & manufacturing preparation
6. **Phase VI (Lessons 39-44):** Hardware assembly, integration & calibration

## Components

### Power Electronics
- **IKW40N120H3:** 1200V 40A IGBT (2× for half-bridge)
- **UCC21550:** Isolated gate driver with hardware dead-time
- **Resonant Tank:** 50µH coil + 470nF capacitor bank (33kHz resonance)

### Auxiliary Power
- **LMR51430:** 24V→5V wide-Vin buck converter (1.1MHz switching)
- **LDO + Pi-filter:** 5V→3.3V low-noise supply for MCU
- **UCC14140-Q1:** 12V→22V/4V isolated dual-output for high-side driver

### Sensing & Control
- **ESP32-S3:** Dual-core MCU with MCPWM peripheral
- **MAX31865:** RTD-to-digital converter (15-bit resolution)
- **ADuM1250:** I2C isolator for probe safety
- **AMC1200:** Isolated current sensing amplifier
- **CT:** Current transformer for tank current monitoring

### Safety & UI
- **Hardware Interlocks:** LM393 comparator for over-temperature/over-current
- **Fan Control:** PWM-controlled active cooling with tachometer feedback
- **User Interface:** Rotary encoder + OLED/LCD display
- **RTD Probe:** Galvanically isolated temperature sensor

### Mechanical/Thermal
- **Heatsinks:** Thermal design for 2kW dissipation
- **Active Cooling:** Temperature-controlled fan
- **Thermal Interface:** Thermal vias, PCB copper pours

## Dependencies

**Software/Tools:**
- KiCad 8.0 (schematic capture, PCB layout, integrated SPICE simulation)
- ngspice (circuit simulation engine)
- ESP-IDF (ESP32 firmware development framework)
- Python 3.x (behavioral modeling, PID tuning scripts)
- Manufacturer SPICE models (IKW40N120H3, UCC21550, LMR51430, UCC14140-Q1)

**Design Constraints:**
- AC Input: 120Vrms 60Hz (US mains)
- DC Bus Voltage: 310V (rectified, with PFC assumed)
- Operating Frequency: 30-40kHz (adjustable for ZVS tuning)
- Target Temperature Control: ±0.5°C steady-state error, zero overshoot
- Isolation Requirements: >10MΩ @ 500V (user-accessible probe)
- Safety Standards: UL/IEC compliance (assumed, not explicitly documented)

**Knowledge Prerequisites:**
- Basic circuit theory (Ohm's law, AC analysis, impedance)
- SPICE simulation fundamentals
- Resonant converter theory (LC tanks, ZVS)
- Microcontroller programming (C/C++)
- PCB design principles (isolation, thermal management)

title: Add EMI filtering to digital control interfaces
description:
Design and verify EMI filtering for ESP32-S3 digital interfaces (PWM, SPI, I2C) to prevent noise coupling from IGBT switching and improve signal integrity. Based on verification work in temper-vkx epic, several interfaces would benefit from additional EMI protection:

1. PWM to UCC21550 (temper-vkx.1): Already has 51Ω + 33pF RC filter, adequate
2. SPI to MAX31865 (temper-vkx.2): Has 33Ω series resistors, provides ~13dB attenuation @ 100MHz
3. I2C through ADUM1250 (temper-vkx.3): 4.7kΩ pull-ups only, no explicit EMI filtering yet

Recommended EMI enhancements:
- Common-mode chokes on I2C lines (if EMC testing shows emissions issues)
- Ferrite beads on power rails (120Ω @ 100MHz) to isolated devices
- Pi-filters on noisy power rails (especially near gate drivers)
- Evaluation of conducted and radiated EMI with spectrum analyzer
- PCB layout review for EMI mitigation (trace routing away from power)

Reference documents:
- sim_11_esp32_max31865_spi_verification.md (SPI noise immunity section)
- sim_10_esp32_ucc21550_pwm_verification.md (RC filter analysis)
- ADUM1250_Documentation.md (Section on EMI and noise immunity)

Priority is low since current designs have basic filtering, but should be validated during hardware bring-up and EMC testing.

description: Simulate AC mains rectification (120/240VAC) with soft-start circuit feeding LMR51430 buck converter input (12-24V range). Verify voltage ripple at rectifier output, soft-start timing, inrush current limiting, and compatibility with LMR51430 input specs. Include decoupling capacitors per LMR51430 datasheet Section 8.2.1.

description: Simulate LMR51430 converting 12-24V to 5V at loads from 100mA to 1.2A (per load budget). Verify output voltage regulation (±2%), load transient response, switching ripple (<50mV), thermal performance (TJ), and loop stability. Include all recommended input/output capacitors and bootstrap components per datasheet Section 8.2.2.

description: Simulate LDO regulator (TBD: select specific part) converting 5V to 3.3V at 380mA input (254mA output per load budget). Verify dropout voltage, line/load regulation, noise performance (<50µV RMS), thermal dissipation, and PSRR. Include input/output decoupling per ESP32-S3 hardware design guidelines.

title: Verify complete aux power chain transient response
description: Simulate complete power-up sequence from AC mains through to stable 3.3V output. Verify power-good sequencing, load step transients (ESP32 WiFi bursts: 150mA baseline to 350mA peak), cross-regulation between 5V and 3.3V rails, and voltage ripple coupling. Confirm all subsystem load requirements are met simultaneously.

title: Level 3: Half-Bridge Power Stage Integration
description: Integrate UCC21550 gate drivers with IKW40N120H3 IGBTs in half-bridge configuration. Verify gate charge delivery, switching waveforms, dead-time adequacy, voltage overshoot suppression, and thermal performance. Include snubber design verification.

title: Verify single IGBT + gate driver switching characteristics
description: Simulate UCC21550 driving single IKW40N120H3 IGBT with resistive load. Measure gate charge delivery waveform, VGE rise/fall times, turn-on/turn-off delays, gate resistor optimization (2.2Ω), and Miller plateau characteristics. Verify gate drive voltage headroom (15V vs 6.5V VGE(th) max). Compare with Lesson 03/04 predictions.

title: Verify half-bridge configuration with dead-time
description: Simulate complete half-bridge: two IKW40N120H3 IGBTs with UCC21550 dual-channel gate drivers. Verify complementary switching with dead-time (700-1000ns), shoot-through prevention, bus voltage handling (310V DC), and switching node voltage excursion (0 to VBUS). Measure dV/dt stress on IGBTs and gate drivers.

title: Verify snubber network design and voltage overshoot suppression
description: Design and simulate RC/RCD snubber networks across IGBTs and half-bridge output. Optimize snubber values to limit voltage overshoot to <20% (372V max from 310V nominal). Verify energy dissipation in snubber resistors, impact on switching losses, and EMI reduction. Reference Lesson 16 design methodology.

title: Verify power stage thermal performance and heatsink requirements
description: Calculate and verify IGBT thermal performance: conduction losses, switching losses, junction temperature under continuous 2kW operation. Verify against Lesson 18 thermal model. Design heatsink requirements (thermal resistance, airflow). Confirm TJ < 150°C with margin under worst-case ambient (85°C).

title: Improve EMI filtering for ESP32-S3 to UCC21550 PWM interface
description:
The current 51Ω + 33pF RC filter provides only 3.26dB attenuation at 100MHz, which may be insufficient in harsh EMI environments near the switch node. Consider design improvements:

**Current Performance:**
- RC filter: R=51Ω, C=33pF (fc ≈ 94MHz)
- 100MHz noise rejection: 3.26dB (31% reduction)
- Verified in sim_10_esp32_ucc21550_pwm_interface.cir

**Options to Evaluate:**
1. Increase capacitance to 47-100pF (fc = 66-33MHz) for better HF rejection
2. Add ferrite bead (50-300Ω @ 100MHz) in series with signal
   - Example: Murata BLM15AG102SN1 (1kΩ @ 100MHz, <1Ω DC)
3. Combination: 51Ω + 47pF + ferrite bead

**Verification Required:**
- SPICE simulation with improved filter designs
- Compare noise rejection @ 100MHz, 200MHz, 500MHz
- Verify rise/fall time impact remains <50ns
- Verify propagation delay increase remains acceptable
- Check GPIO loading stays within limits

**Trade-offs:**
- Higher capacitance: Better HF filtering but slower edges
- Ferrite bead: Excellent HF filtering, minimal timing impact, adds cost/BOM

**Trigger for Implementation:**
- Hardware testing shows EMI issues or false triggering
- EMC compliance testing reveals excessive emissions
- Operation near switch node requires extra immunity

**Reference:**
- Original verification: simulation/results/sim_10_esp32_ucc21550_pwm_verification.md (Section 5: Noise Immunity)
- Parent task: temper-vkx.1 (completed)

title: Firmware HAL Abstraction and TDD Infrastructure
description:
Implement Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL) and Test-Driven Development infrastructure for the Temper induction cooker firmware. This enables unit testing of all control logic (state machine, PID, safety, pan detection, PLL) without hardware dependencies.

**Goals:**
1. Abstract all hardware access through HAL interfaces (GPIO, ADC, PWM, SPI, Timer)
2. Create mock implementations for host-based testing
3. Implement comprehensive unit tests using ESP-IDF Unity framework
4. Verify firmware behavior against design specifications

**Key Specifications to Test Against:**
- OCP threshold: 50A ±5A (2.5V at comparator)
- OVP threshold: 390V (3.55V after divider)
- Thermal shutdown: 85°C IGBT junction
- Watchdog timeout: 1.6s
- Temperature precision: ±0.5°C
- Switching frequency: 38-50 kHz
- Dead-time: 500ns minimum
- Pan detection: valid 3-15A, no-pan >20A, small object <3A

**Deliverables:**
- HAL interface headers in firmware/components/hal/include/
- Mock implementations in firmware/components/hal/mock/
- ESP32 implementations in firmware/components/hal/esp32/
- Test suite in firmware/test/
- All tests passing against design specifications

title: State machine tests and fixes
description:
Write comprehensive tests for state_machine.c and fix any failures:

**States to test:**
- STATE_INIT → STATE_IDLE (on successful init)
- STATE_IDLE → STATE_PAN_DETECT (on start command)

title: Pan detection tests and fixes
description:
Write comprehensive tests for pan_detection.c and fix any failures:

**Pan detection thresholds (from RESONANT_TANK_DESIGN.md):**
- Valid pan: 3-15A coil current
- No pan: >20A (resonant tank unloaded)
- Small object: <3A (insufficient coupling)
- Ferromagnetic: Look for expected impedance pattern

**Functionality to test:**
1. Valid pan detection (3-15A range)
2. No pan detection (>20A)
3. Small object rejection (<3A)
4. Debouncing - single glitch doesn't trigger
5. Hysteresis - no oscillation at boundaries
6. Pan removal during heating - proper shutdown
7. Calibration support - adjustable thresholds

**Failure modes to test:**
1. No debouncing causing false triggers
2. Hardcoded thresholds vs calibratable
3. Missing hysteresis causing oscillation
4. Not distinguishing pan removal vs small object
5. Timeout if no decision reached

**Test scenarios:**
- Inject 10A reading → expect VALID_PAN
- Inject 25A reading → expect NO_PAN
- Inject 1A reading → expect SMALL_OBJECT
- Inject alternating 14A/16A → verify no oscillation (hysteresis)

title: Epic: Resonant Tank and Pan Load Simulation

KEY CHALLENGE - VARIABLE LOAD:
Pan coupling varies based on material, size, and air gap, causing:
- Inductance shift (affecting resonant frequency)
- Reflected resistance change (affecting power delivery)
- Coupling coefficient variation (k = 0.2 to 0.6 typical)

title: Epic: Hardware Safety Interlock Design and Verification
description:
Design and verify all hardware safety interlocks required for safe induction cooker operation. This epic MUST be completed before any power stage testing.

SAFETY PHILOSOPHY:
Hardware interlocks provide fail-safe protection independent of firmware. Even if the ESP32 crashes, locks up, or has a bug, hardware interlocks must disable the power stage within <1µs.

REQUIRED INTERLOCKS:
1. Overcurrent Protection (OCP) - Trip at 50A peak (125% of rated)
2. Overvoltage Protection (OVP) - Trip at 400V DC bus (110% of nominal)
3. Undervoltage Lockout (UVLO) - Already in UCC21550B, verified in temper-8l2
4. Gate Driver Fault - Fast disable path from comparator to UCC21550 DISABLE pin
5. Thermal Shutdown - Hardware comparator on IGBT heatsink NTC

RESPONSE TIME REQUIREMENT:
- OCP: <1µs from detection to gate driver disable
- OVP: <10µs (slower, bus capacitor provides some hold time)
- Thermal: <100ms (thermal mass provides time)

DESIGN APPROACH:
- Use fast comparators (LM339 or TLV3202 for speed)
- Direct hardware path to UCC21550 DISABLE pin
- OR-gate combining all fault sources
- Latching behavior (requires manual reset after fault)
- LED indicators for fault diagnosis

DELIVERABLES:
1. sim_17_ocp_protection.cir - OCP circuit simulation
2. sim_18_ovp_protection.cir - OVP circuit simulation
3. sim_19_thermal_shutdown.cir - NTC comparator simulation
4. sim_20_interlock_integration.cir - Combined fault logic
5. SAFETY_INTERLOCK_DESIGN.md - Complete design document

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All simulations pass with documented response times
- Response times meet requirements with 2x margin
- Fault conditions tested: overcurrent, overvoltage, overtemp, gate driver UVLO
- Reset and indication logic verified

BLOCKS:
- temper-gqb (Half-Bridge Power Stage) - Cannot test safely without interlocks
- temper-axx (Resonant Tank) - Cannot test safely without interlocks

CT REQUIREMENTS:
- Primary: 1 turn (tank wire passes through CT core)
- Secondary: 1000 turns (typical, gives 1000:1 ratio)
- Rated current: 50A primary → 50mA secondary
- Frequency range: 20kHz - 100kHz (induction cooker switching range)
- Accuracy: ±1% for power measurement, ±10% for OCP (conservative trip)

CT MODEL PARAMETERS:
- Turns ratio: N = 1000:1
- Magnetizing inductance: Lm = 10mH (typical for ferrite core)
- Leakage inductance: Ll = 100µH (<<Lm)
- Winding resistance: Rw = 50Ω (secondary)

title: Design overcurrent protection (OCP) comparator circuit
description:
Design hardware OCP circuit using current transformer output to detect overcurrent and disable gate drivers within <1µs.

CIRCUIT REQUIREMENTS:
- Input: CT secondary voltage (proportional to tank current)
- Threshold: 50A peak (set by voltage divider on comparator reference)
- Output: Logic-level signal to fault OR-gate
- Response time: <500ns comparator + <500ns gate driver disable = <1µs total

DESIGN APPROACH:
1. CT burden resistor sized for 50A → ~2.5V
2. Fast comparator (TLV3202: 40ns propagation)
3. Reference voltage from resistor divider
4. Hysteresis resistor to prevent chatter
5. Open-collector output for OR-gate

SIMULATION: sim_17_ocp_protection.cir
DELIVERABLES: OCP circuit, component selection, trip threshold calculation

title: Design overvoltage protection (OVP) comparator circuit
description:
Design hardware OVP circuit to detect DC bus overvoltage (>400V) and disable gate drivers.

CIRCUIT REQUIREMENTS:
- Input: Isolated DC bus voltage sense (resistive divider + optocoupler)
- Threshold: 400V DC (trip point), 380V reset (hysteresis)
- Output: Logic-level signal to fault OR-gate
- Response time: <10µs

ISOLATION APPROACH:
- High-value resistive divider (3x 1MΩ) + optocoupler for isolation
- Divider: 310V → 3.1V using 3MΩ/30kΩ (100:1 ratio)
- Comparator on isolated side for clean logic output

SIMULATION: sim_18_ovp_protection.cir
DELIVERABLES: OVP circuit, isolation boundary documentation

title: Design thermal shutdown comparator circuit
description:
Design hardware thermal protection using NTC thermistor on IGBT heatsink.

CIRCUIT REQUIREMENTS:
- Input: NTC thermistor (10kΩ @ 25°C typical) on IGBT heatsink
- Trip threshold: 85°C (conservative, IGBT rated to 150°C Tj)
- Reset threshold: 75°C (hysteresis prevents cycling)
- Response time: <100ms (thermal mass provides time)

NTC BEHAVIOR:
- At 25°C: 10kΩ, At 85°C: ~2.5kΩ
- Voltage divider with pullup resistor
- Comparator trips when voltage falls below threshold

SIMULATION: sim_19_thermal_shutdown.cir
DELIVERABLES: Thermal circuit, NTC selection, calibration procedure

title: Design fault integration OR-gate and latching logic
description:
Design the fault combination and latching logic that feeds the UCC21550 DISABLE pin.

CIRCUIT REQUIREMENTS:
- Inputs: OCP_FAULT, OVP_FAULT, THERMAL_FAULT, (optional: ESP32_WDT_FAULT)
- Output: GATE_DISABLE signal to UCC21550 DISABLE pin (active HIGH disables)
- Behavior: Latching (any fault latches output HIGH until manual reset)
- Indication: LED per fault source for diagnosis

LATCHING REQUIREMENT:
Once a fault occurs, the system must remain disabled until:
1. Fault condition clears
2. User presses RESET button

IMPLEMENTATION: Comparator with positive feedback (self-latching)
